comparisons.kpi_name: Does the company have an AI strategy?
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Formal governance structure
comparisons.ground_truth_answer:
iliad SA’s AI oversight is embedded in its existing governance structure:
- Board-level CSR Committee (members: Bertile Burel, Virginie Calmels; chaired by Jacques Veyrat) overseeing ESG impacts relevant to AI and data-center operations, supported by a CSR Steering Committee.
- Audit Committee overseeing risk management, internal control and internal audit, which includes technology/AI risks.
- Data Protection governance led by a Chief Data Protection Officer with a network of DPOs to ensure GDPR-compliant AI lifecycle management (e.g., DPIAs, records of processing, data-subject rights).
- Ethics & Compliance function (Code of Ethics, anti‑corruption program, and whistleblowing channel) to enforce responsible conduct around AI uses.
- Green Finance Committee (Treasury, Sustainability/CSR and Finance leadership) that evaluates and monitors projects, including energy‑intensive AI/data‑center investments.
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: The claims indicate (1) a board-level CSR Committee created in late 2022 (members: B. Burel, V. Calmels; chair: J. Veyrat) that oversees ESG matters relevant to AI/data centers; (2) an Audit Committee responsible for risk management and internal audit, covering emerging tech/AI risks; (3) a Data Protection governance system led by a Chief DPO with a network of DPOs for GDPR compliance; (4) an Ethics & Compliance framework with a Code of Ethics, anti-corruption program, and whistleblowing; and (5) a Green Finance Committee including Treasury, Sustainability/CSR, and Finance leaders to evaluate and monitor eligible projects, notably data center/AI infrastructure. These bodies collectively provide oversight for AI strategy, compliance, and lifecycle as described in the provided claims.

comparisons.kpi_name: Percentage of AI initiatives with outcomes tied to measurable business KPIs.
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of AI use cases with quantitative impact since 2023
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Identified two AI use cases with quantified outcomes since 2023: (1) an AI-based energy optimization system that cut electricity consumption per GB to 0.095 kWh by end-2023 (from 0.190 kWh), and (2) AI-driven RF band switching that reduces base station electricity consumption by more than 10% during off-periods. Other initiatives (e.g., Mistral AI partnership, GPU capacity expansion) lack disclosed quantitative impact metrics tied to a specific AI use case.

comparisons.kpi_name: Presence of AI talent/experience on the Board
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of AI talents
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: I identified 34 unique employees associated with iliad SA or subsidiaries currently in AI-related roles as defined: AI Development, Data Engineering, AI Software Implementation, and AI Product Management. I counted only individuals with confirmed, current position at iliad SA (including key subsidiaries like Free, Scaleway, and Play) and excluded any duplicates or ambiguous entries. The roles were matched strictly to the provided categories, and all included individuals had clear job functions mapped to AI or data/analytics as per the definition.

comparisons.kpi_name: Number of use cases  and alignment with strategic priorities
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of AI use cases with financial impact since 2023
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: The analysis enumerates eight AI use cases since 2023 that have financial impact: (1) technician intervention optimization, (2) subscriber needs forecasting and CLV optimization, (3) internal process optimization, (4) developer copilot for productivity, (5) customer care/training accelerator, (6) AI-based energy optimization, (7) customer AI assistant (le Chat Pro), and (8) open-source AI model development driving competitive/financial benefits. It explicitly concludes there are “at least eight distinct” AI use cases with documented financial impact.

comparisons.kpi_name: When were the first AI solutions executed?
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of AI related acquisitions since 2023
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: After strict filtering for unique AI-related acquisitions by Iliad SA or its subsidiaries since 2023, five distinct companies were identified: ITrust (AI-based cybersecurity, acquired April 2023), Saagie (AI/data platform, acquired June 2025 by Scaleway), Vestigit (AI-watermarking, acquired June 2023 via Redge Technologies, owned by Iliad's Play), Kyutai (AI lab co-founded in November 2023), and OpCore (data center platform, acquired in partnership in 2025; clearly linked to AI infrastructure). Other entries such as investments, internal developments, or general AI programs were excluded as they did not fit the definition of acquiring another company to enhance AI capabilities. Duplicates across sources were consolidated and subsidiary-parent relationships normalized. No ambiguous or unsupported acquisitions were counted.

comparisons.kpi_name: Investments in AI-focused companies
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of AI investments since 2023
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Four distinct AI-related investments by Iliad SA (or its subsidiaries/founder) since 2023 were identified and validated: (1) Scaleway's acquisition of Saagie (2025), (2) a strategic partnership and investment with GENCI/CNRS for AI infrastructure (2025), (3) partnership with InfraVia to develop OpCore, a hyperscale AI-focused data center platform (2024/2025), and (4) investment in Hugging Face by Xavier Niel (2023). Each event was cross-checked for AI relevance, date, and direct company involvement. One data point regarding consolidation with Telecom Italia and CVC Capital Partners was excluded due to unclear evidence of a concrete AI investment by Iliad SA, as it focused on a potential telecom deal rather than a direct AI-related investment.

comparisons.kpi_name: Partnerships with AI companies
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Name of partnerships
comparisons.ground_truth_answer: Mistral AI, Kyutai, InfraVia, Scaleway, NVIDIA, H, HPE, Dell Technologies, AMD, VAST Data, ITrust, Kaliop, CMES, GENCI, CNRS, Saagie, DeepSeek, Galene.AI, Poolside AI, Amazon (Alexa), Redge Technologies, France Télévisions, Quandela, GSMA, Allianz Accelerator, Ampere Computing, Hugging Face, AI Factory France, Jaguar Network, Adspective, University of Rennes (Le Chat Universités)
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: I listed all unique organizations with direct, official AI-related partnerships (including research labs, hyperscale infrastructure, AI compute, startups, industry consortia, university collaborations, and enterprise partners) involving Iliad SA or its subsidiaries, consolidating subsidiaries or recurring partners under a single canonical name and excluding ambiguous/duplicate entries. Each listed entity is supported by direct evidence of formal partnership or invested collaboration relevant to AI maturity. I excluded pure customers, investors without partnership models, and items with unclear Iliad SA connection. Where partnerships operate via subsidiaries (e.g., Scaleway), the unique counterparty name is retained.

comparisons.kpi_name: Job postings- number of open positions advertised in the public domain
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of publicly advertised in HR/job postings?
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: After validating all entries, 40 unique AI-related job postings affiliated with iliad SA or its subsidiaries (such as Scaleway, Free, Play, ITrust, Opcore, Kyutai, iliad Italia/Poland) are identified. Duplicates, non-relevant/ambiguous entries, and those lacking both explicit AI tasks and an organizational link to iliad were excluded. Each remaining posting was confirmed as both AI-relevant and affiliated with iliad SA during the deduplication and validation process (e.g., Iliad, Scaleway, Free, Play, Kyutai, ITrust).

comparisons.kpi_name: AI Team Leadership structure- number of leaders (percentage of Job Titles)
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of Board members with AI experience
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Based on the provided relationship data and detailed analysis, there are currently zero individuals serving on iliad SA's official Board of Directors with proven AI expertise. The board consists of 11 members with general technology and telecommunications backgrounds, but none have demonstrable, hands-on AI/ML or data science leadership credentials. The only executive with direct AI responsibility (Aude Durand) is not a board member and thus does not count toward this metric. This conclusion is directly supported by the skills matrix and individual director profiles referenced in the company's official disclosures.

comparisons.kpi_name: AI Team Leadership structure- number of leaders (in number)
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: Number of data breaches in past 5 years
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: The claims report a single confirmed incident in early October 2024: a cyberattack targeting a CRM system that led to unauthorized access to some subscriber personal data. Iliad notified CNIL and ANSSI and informed affected customers, and filed a criminal complaint [4][3]. No other data breaches are mentioned in the provided claims for the past five years, so the count is 1 based on available evidence.

comparisons.kpi_name: AI Ethics role appointed at company, AI risk and management
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: AI Ethics documentation
comparisons.ground_truth_answer: No dedicated AI ethics documentation identified. Iliad’s available materials focus on general business ethics and compliance (Code of Ethics updated from 2021 with a 2025 revision, Anti‑Corruption Code of Conduct, conflicts‑of‑interest brochure, Gifts & Hospitality/Invitations policies, whistleblowing systems) and data protection/GDPR policies, but the claims do not mention AI‑specific ethics or Responsible AI guidelines.
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: The claims describe a comprehensive business ethics framework: a Code of Ethics (2021, update scheduled for 2025), Anti‑Corruption Code of Conduct, conflicts‑of‑interest guidelines, and Gifts & Hospitality/Invitations policies, plus whistleblowing systems and GDPR/data protection policies [2][3][6][13]. None of the provided claims reference AI‑specific ethics or Responsible AI documentation, so based on the evidence, Iliad has ethics documentation but not AI‑targeted ethics guidelines in the claims set.

comparisons.kpi_name: Compliance with AI regulations
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: workplace reduction in FTEs
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: All available evidence shows that iliad SA has not reported any workforce reduction (either absolute or percentage) explicitly attributed to automation or AI adoption. In fact, the company's headcount increased by 517 employees in 2024 and by 1,127 in 2023, with total staff reaching 18,234 at the end of 2024. No sources or company statements link any FTE reduction to AI or automation; instead, the workforce is growing despite major AI investments. Therefore, the indicative value for workforce reduction due to AI is 0%.

comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: The claims describe a comprehensive business ethics framework: a Code of Ethics (2021, update scheduled for 2025), Anti‑Corruption Code of Conduct, conflicts‑of‑interest guidelines, and Gifts & Hospitality/Invitations policies, plus whistleblowing systems and GDPR/data protection policies [2][3][6][13]. None of the provided claims reference AI‑specific ethics or Responsible AI documentation, so based on the evidence, Iliad has ethics documentation but not AI‑targeted ethics guidelines in the claims set.

comparisons.kpi_name: Governance documents published on AI deployment
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback scoring based on evidence text.
comparisons.ground_truth_name: First year of AI deployment
comparisons.ground_truth_answer: 2023 — In September 2023, Iliad (via its Scaleway subsidiary) deployed an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD at Datacenter 5 near Paris—comprising 127 DGX H100 systems (1,016 H100 GPUs)—which at the time was billed as Europe’s most powerful cloud‑native AI supercomputer. Following this, Scaleway began marketing a full suite of cloud‑native AI services to customers, marking Iliad’s first operational‑scale AI deployment.
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Multiple company statements document that Iliad’s first at‑scale AI deployment occurred in 2023: on Sep 26, 2023 the Group announced the installation of an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD at DC5, described as the most powerful cloud‑native AI supercomputer deployed in Europe at that date [21]. The 2023/2024 reporting further specifies the configuration—127 DGX H100 systems totaling 1,016 H100 GPUs—and notes that Scaleway subsequently commercialized a full suite of cloud‑native AI offerings for customers, evidencing operational use rather than a pilot [5][6].

unmatched_kpis: C-level statements about AI Strategy
AI communications in social media, white papers (volume and boldness of messaging), limit to G7 or primary markets
Presence and clarity of a publicly communicated AI roadmap outlining long-term objectives, milestones, and investment priorities.
To what extent does the organization allocate and scale financial, human, and technological resources to AI initiatives in a way that delivers measurable business outcomes?
Type of technology, AI model (include agentic AI) point in time implementation or agents Maturity of agentic workforce within the operating model.
Has implementation been primarily an in-house development or partnership model? In-house or outsource some risk, external expertise

To what extent has the company achieved measurable workforce efficiency through AI or GenAI — such as automation, task augmentation, or optimized hiring — resulting in stable or increased output with equal or fewer FTEs?
To what extent has the company operationalized AI — demonstrating tangible rollout and active usage of AI or GenAI in core business operations, workflows, or decision processes at scale?

To what extent has the company maintained data security and confidentiality in its AI operations — including the presence or absence of data breaches, leaks, or other security incidents over the past five years?
To what extent has the company established a mature, enterprise-wide data strategy and foundational architecture that effectively supports AI development and deployment?
To what extent does the company integrate and harmonize data from diverse internal and external sources to make it efficiently usable for AI and analytics applications?
To what extent is the company’s hardware and cloud infrastructure capable of handling expanding AI workloads with high responsiveness, scalability, and cost-efficiency?
To what extent has the company adopted modern, enterprise-grade analytics and AI platforms that streamline model development, deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management?
To what extent are the company’s processes and tools for developing, deploying, monitoring, and improving ML models mature, automated, and aligned with production standards?
To what extent has the company implemented robust mechanisms that safeguard data against unauthorized access or misuse, ensuring compliance with privacy and security regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA)?
To what extent is the company modernizing or integrating legacy systems into its current data and AI architecture to eliminate bottlenecks and data silos?
To what extent does the company explore and adopt complementary frontier technologies (e.g., IoT, blockchain, edge computing, AR/VR, quantum computing) that enhance or expand AI capabilities?
Training- disclosed training efforts and breadth of the activity (public disclosure)

Job titles- number of employees in AI Development, Data Engineering, AI Software implementation, AI product management (other roles to be discussed) - Experience description demonstrates AI skill base or focus (percentage of total employees)

Job titles- number of employees in AI Development, Data Engineering, AI Software implementation, AI product management (other roles to be discussed) - Experience description demonstrates AI skill base or focus (in number)
What department “owns” AI strategy? Do they have a tech department that has its own Board member?
To what extent does the company actively support employee well-being and engagement throughout its AI transformation journey?
How effectively does the organization foster international and multicultural collaboration in AI development and deployment?
How strongly is an AI-driven and innovation-oriented mindset embedded in the company’s organizational culture?
How mature and effective are the company’s change management and communication practices in enabling AI adoption?
How well are cross-functional collaboration and governance structures established to coordinate AI initiatives across departments?
To what degree has the company built AI literacy and encouraged knowledge sharing across the workforce?
How ready is the company’s culture to embrace data-driven decision-making and continuous AI-enabled improvement?
How does the company integrate community engagement and social responsibility into its AI initiatives?
To what extent does the company promote an ethical culture and mechanisms for continuous improvement in AI practices?
Open source- overall engagement with open source development ecosystem, code repositories, open source projects (i.e. GitHub)
How explicitly does AI enhance the customer value proposition (e.g., personalization, NPS, retention) and tie to growth/margin outcomes?
Evidence that AI strategy targets disruptive differentiation (new AI‑native offerings, pricing models, or channels) beyond efficiency.
How clear are cross‑functional roles and responsibilities for AI (business, product, data, risk, legal) from idea to run?
How systematically are external benchmarks used to drive AI delivery performance (speed, quality, cost) and improvement targets?
What business‑visible monitoring exists for models in production (performance, drift, data quality, incidents) and who sees it?
Is there a standardized, auditable methodology to estimate and realize ROI for AI (ex‑ante and ex‑post)?
How well is delivery aligned with applicable AI/sector regulations during rollout and operation (e.g., DPIA, model cards, DSARs)?
How mature and reproducible is the model development workflow (from experimentation to CI/CD and release)?
Do users have a governed self‑service data & AI portal/marketplace (catalog, access control, usage analytics)?
Is there an enterprise‑wide canonical data model that underpins AI/analytics across domains?
How complete and enforced are data & model and policies/standards (quality, lineage, privacy, retention, access)?
To what extent is metadata (technical + business) captured and used to improve discovery, lineage, and MDM?
Is risk appetite for AI explicitly defined (by domain/use case) and used for approvals and guardrails?
How visible and concrete is the company’s public advocacy and action on responsible AI (AI‑for‑Good)?
Are role‑based learning paths and curricula established for AI (from execs to engineers) with certification?
How structured and scalable is experimentation (PoCs/pilots) with clear success metrics and governance?
Are there structured alliances/JVs to accelerate AI value with shared IP, data, or distribution?
Do standardized, privacy‑preserving data‑sharing mechanisms exist across the ecosystem?
Is the firm actively shaping AI standards and regulation via consortia, position papers, and pilots?
How rigorous is partner/vendor capability assessment (security, compliance, quality, delivery, integration)?

2 year revenue growth CAGR for latest fiscal year
AI ethics governance model yes or no
